# Commit Message Instructions

## Format

Commit messages must follow this format:

```
<type> <emoji>: <short description>
```

For example: `fix рҹҗӣ: resolve incorrect bearing calculation in lidar_geometry`

The description should be concise and written in the imperative mood (e.g. "add", "fix", "remove", not "added", "fixed", "removed").

---

## Commit Types and Emojis

| Type | Emoji | When to use |
|------|-------|-------------|
| `feat` | вңЁ | Introduce a new feature |
| `fix` | рҹҗӣ | Fix a bug |
| `hotfix` | рҹҡ‘пёҸ | Critical hotfix |
| `docs` | рҹ“қ | Add or update documentation |
| `style` | рҹ’„ | Improve code structure or formatting (no logic change) |
| `refactor` | вҷ»пёҸ | Refactor code without fixing a bug or adding a feature |
| `perf` | вҡЎпёҸ | Improve performance |
| `test` | вң… | Add, update, or fix tests |
| `config` | рҹ”§ | Add or update configuration files |
| `deps` | рҹҡј | Dependency change |
| `chore` | рҹ”Ё | Add or update development scripts or tooling |
| `move` | рҹҡҡ | Move or rename files or paths |
| `types` | рҹҸ·пёҸ | Add or update type annotations |
| `log` | рҹ”Ҡ | Update logs |
| `cleanup` | рҹ—‘пёҸ | Remove code or files |
| `comments` | рҹ’Ў | Add or update comments in source code |
| `gitignore` | рҹҷҲ | Add or update .gitignore |
| `catch` | рҹҘ… | Catch errors |
